---
title: "taxodist"
subtitle: "Taxonomic Distance and Phylogenetic Lineage Computation"
---

<!-- Badges -->
<p align="left">
  <a href="https://cran.r-project.org/package=taxodist">
    <img src="https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/taxodist" alt="CRAN status">
  </a>
  <a href="https://pypi.org/project/taxodist/">
    <img src="https://badge.fury.io/py/taxodist.svg" alt="PyPI Version">
  </a>
</p>

## Overview

**`taxodist`** provides a unified computational framework in R and Python for calculating phylogenetic distances between any two taxa. It leverages the deeply resolved hierarchical lineage data from [The Taxonomicon](http://taxonomicon.taxonomy.nl), a comprehensive curated classification of all life based on *Systema Naturae 2000*.

Given two taxon names, the package retrieves their full lineages, identifies the most recent common ancestor (MRCA), and computes a dissimilarity index based on lineage depth:

$$
d(A, B) = \frac{1}{\text{depth}(\text{MRCA}(A,B))}
$$

The deeper the shared ancestor, the smaller (closer to zero) the distance. This tree-metric ensures that taxa diverging at the same node are always equidistant from any third taxon, regardless of downstream lineage depth differences.

## Key Features

* **Phylogenetic Computation:** Pairwise taxonomic distances, MRCA identification, and precise taxonomic path tracing.
* **Multivariate Ecology:** Native generation of symmetric distance matrices ready for hierarchical clustering (`taxo_cluster`), Principal Coordinate Analysis (`taxo_ordinate`), and direct integration with `vegan` (R) or `scipy` (Python).
* **Robust Data Retrieval:** Built-in session-level caching to minimize network overhead, automatic homonym disambiguation via strict numeric IDs, and graceful fallback handling for upstream server timeouts.

## Quick Start

The API is strictly equivalent across supported languages, ensuring full reproducibility in multi-language bioinformatics workflows.

::: {.panel-tabset}

### R

```r
# Install from CRAN
install.packages("taxodist")

library(taxodist)

# Compute pairwise distance
taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor")

# Generate a distance matrix
theropods <- c("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor", "Spinosaurus", "Carnotaurus")
mat <- distance_matrix(theropods)
```

### Python

```python
# Install from PyPI
pip install taxodist

import taxodist as td

# Compute pairwise distance
td.taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor")

# Generate a distance matrix
theropods = ["Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor", "Spinosaurus", "Carnotaurus"]
mat = td.distance_matrix(theropods)
```
:::
